prompt: Add TTL (time-to-live) support to the key-value store so entries expire after a configurable duration

turns.content:
<task>
Add TTL (time-to-live) support to the key-value store so entries expire after a configurable duration
</task>

arguments.summary: Read KV store with sync.RWMutex-protected map. Has Set/Get/Delete. Need to add TTL: store expiration time with each entry, check on Get, add SetWithTTL method.

next_steps.description: Change data map to store entry struct with value and expiresAt time.Time, add SetWithTTL(key, value, ttl) method
Update Get to check expiration and auto-delete expired entries, test TTL behavior

turns.content:
=== RUN   TestSetAndGet
--- PASS: TestSetAndGet (0.00s)
=== RUN   TestDelete
--- PASS: TestDelete (0.00s)
=== RUN   TestTTLExpiry
--- PASS: TestTTLExpiry (0.11s)
PASS

arguments.summary: Added TTL support: entry struct with value/expiresAt/hasTTL, SetWithTTL method, Get checks expiration and auto-deletes expired entries. Set without TTL still works. All 3 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer:
Added TTL support to KV store in pkg/kvdb/store.go:
- entry struct with expiresAt timestamp and hasTTL flag
- SetWithTTL(key, value, duration) sets entry with expiration
- Get() auto-deletes and returns false for expired entries
- Set() without TTL still works (no expiration)
All tests pass.
